  • About

  • Contact

Musician & Multi-Instrumentalist

TayaBrownbill

A multi-instrumentalist with a heart in woodwind — clarinet, saxophone, flute and beyond. Heading to the University of York in autumn 2026 to explore where music, people, and wellbeing meet.

Get in touch Learn more

About

Music as a way of connecting

I started playing piano at three years old, and music has been at the centre of everything since. Over the years that early love grew into something much wider — clarinet, saxophone, singing, bass guitar, and a deep fascination with what music does to people.

It was a day spent with Our Dementia Choir, as part of a placement with Nottinghamshire's Inspire Music, that gave me real clarity on the direction I want to go. Watching music reach people in ways that words can't — that stayed with me. It's shaped everything from the modules I'm excited to study at York, to the work I do now as a teaching assistant.

I'm a sixth-former at The Minster School, Southwell, heading to the University of York in autumn 2026 to study Music — with an interest in music psychology, ethnomusicology, and the ways music intersects with health and wellbeing.

Key strengths

  • Natural leader & ensemble conductor
  • Warm, people-focused communicator
  • Strong sight-reader across instruments
  • Dedicated & highly reliable
  • Analytical — music theory & musicology

Ensembles & roles

  • Training Orchestra
  • Jazz Band
  • Concert Band
  • Pit band — four school musicals
  • Performing Arts Leader (Yr 12–13)

"After speaking to members of Our Dementia Choir, it fundamentally changed my perspective on the impact of music — I've always wanted a career related to music, teaching, and helping others, but not until having those conversations did I truly realise how important this was."

Personal Statement, 2025

Instruments

Playing across many voices

Rooted in woodwind — clarinet, baritone sax, tenor sax, flute — and extending across piano, voice, and bass guitar. Taya brings genuine versatility to every ensemble she joins, with a particular gift for coaxing warmth and grace from the bigger, brasher horns.

Clarinet Working towards Grade 8 · Summer 2026

Grade 6 Merit (ABRSM). Pit band across four school musicals. Performed in Minster masterclass with Jess Gillam.

Piano Working towards Grade 8 · Autumn 2026

Grade 6 Merit (ABRSM). School piano competition winner twice. Playing since age three.

Saxophone Alto · Tenor · Bari · Self-taught extended

Learnt Tenor Sax and Baritone Sax to Grade 6+ standard for Shrek the Musical. Community and ensemble performance.

Voice Grade 5 Merit (ABRSM)

Sings tenor and alto in choral works. Annual Handel's Messiah performer at Southwell Minster.

Bass Guitar Self-taught

Arranged and performed in a skiffle band for One Man Two Guvnors — entirely student-led.

Music Theory Grade 5 Distinction (ABRSM)

Strong theoretical foundation underpinning all performance and compositional work.

Experience

Where music meets people

July 2025 — Present

Volunteer Teaching Assistant

Inspire Culture — Nottingham Music Hub, Newark on Trent

Supporting professional instrumental tutors delivering music education to children and young people across Nottinghamshire. Assists with lessons, demonstrates technique, and provides one-to-one encouragement. Spent a formative day with Our Dementia Choir — observing first-hand the impact of music on mental health and memory. Consistently praised by Inspire staff for warmth, reliability, and a natural way with learners.

April 2023

School Work Experience — Teaching Assistant

Lowe's Wong Junior School, Southwell

Supported classroom teachers with primary-age pupils across small-group activities, reading support, and classroom routines. Confirmed a genuine enjoyment of working with children and reinforced the interest in a teaching-related career.

2022 — 2023

Community Volunteer

Reach Learning, Southwell

Regular community volunteering including horticultural and kitchen maintenance tasks. Achieved five-star feedback from the site manager for reliability and attitude.

Ongoing

Ensemble Leader & Performing Arts Lead

The Minster School, Southwell

Conducts and leads three school ensembles — a training orchestra, a jazz band, and a concert band. As Performing Arts Leader, organises ticket sales and coordinates competitions for junior students. Pit musician across four school musicals, including learning three new instruments to performance standard for Shrek the Musical.

Education

Qualifications & training

A Levels · Predicted

Music (A) · English Lit (A) · Drama (A)

The Minster School, Southwell · 2024–2026

Analytical depth across three creative disciplines — musicology and score analysis, critical interpretation, and directing in drama.

GCSEs · 2024

9 English · 8 Maths · 8 Science · 8 Music

The Minster School, Southwell

Strong across sciences and humanities alongside the arts. Full suite including Drama (7), Spanish (7), RE (7), and Further Maths (6).

ABRSM · Clarinet

Grade 6 Merit — working towards Grade 8

Target: Summer 2026

Primary instrument. Performed in Minster masterclass with saxophonist Jess Gillam. Extensive ensemble and pit band experience across multiple genres.

ABRSM · Piano

Grade 6 Merit — working towards Grade 8

Target: Autumn 2026

Playing since age three. Won the school piano competition twice.

ABRSM · Theory

Music Theory Grade 5 — Distinction

ABRSM, 2021

Strong theoretical foundation supporting A-level musicology and score analysis.

University

BMus Music — University of York

Commencing Autumn 2026

Looking forward to modules in music psychology and ethnomusicology — exploring how music connects with health, wellbeing, and community.

Get in touch

Let's connect

Whether you're looking for a committed young musician, a teaching assistant with a natural warmth for learners, or simply want to say hello — I'd love to hear from you.

tjbrownbill@icloud.com
LinkedIn Nottinghamshire, UK Instagram

--text:#28211a; --text-mid:#6b5f55; --text-light:#a8998e; --accent:#7a5c3a; --accent-warm:#c4956a; --accent-pale:#eddec9; --serif:'Cormorant Garamond',Georgia,serif; --sans:'Jost',system-ui,sans-serif; --rule:rgba(120,90,60,0.15); } html{scroll-behavior:smooth} body{font-family:var(--sans);background:var(--bg);color:var(--text);font-weight:300;line-height:1.7;overflow-x:hidden} /* NAV */ nav{position:fixed;top:0;width:100%;z-index:100;padding:1.6rem 3rem;display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;transition:background 0.4s,padding 0.4s} nav.scrolled{background:rgba(247,244,239,0.94);backdrop-filter:blur(16px);padding:0.9rem 3rem;border-bottom:0.5px solid var(--rule)} .nav-logo{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:1.05rem;font-weight:400;letter-spacing:0.05em;color:var(--text);text-decoration:none;opacity:0;transition:opacity 0.3s} nav.scrolled .nav-logo{opacity:1} .nav-links{display:flex;gap:2.5rem;list-style:none} .nav-links a{font-size:0.72rem;letter-spacing:0.2em;text-transform:uppercase;text-decoration:none;color:var(--text-mid);transition:color 0.2s} .nav-links a:hover{color:var(--accent)} /* HERO */ .hero{min-height:100vh;display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;align-items:center;overflow:hidden;position:relative} .hero-left{padding:8rem 4rem 6rem 5vw;display:flex;flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;z-index:2} .hero-eyebrow{font-size:0.7rem;letter-spacing:0.28em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent-warm);margin-bottom:2rem;opacity:0;animation:fadeUp 0.9s 0.3s ease forwards} .hero h1{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(3.8rem,7vw,7rem);font-weight:300;line-height:0.95;letter-spacing:-0.02em;opacity:0;animation:fadeUp 1s 0.55s ease forwards} .hero h1 em{font-style:italic;display:block;color:var(--accent)} .hero-tagline{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(1rem,1.6vw,1.3rem);font-style:italic;font-weight:300;color:var(--text-mid);margin-top:2rem;max-width:380px;line-height:1.6;opacity:0;animation:fadeUp 1s 0.8s ease forwards} .hero-cta{display:flex;gap:1.5rem;margin-top:3rem;opacity:0;animation:fadeUp 1s 1.05s ease forwards} .btn-primary{font-size:0.72rem;letter-spacing:0.2em;text-transform:uppercase;text-decoration:none;color:var(--cream);background:var(--accent);padding:0.85rem 2rem;transition:background 0.2s,transform 0.2s} .btn-primary:hover{background:var(--text);transform:translateY(-1px)} .btn-secondary{font-size:0.72rem;letter-spacing:0.2em;text-transform:uppercase;text-decoration:none;color:var(--accent);border-bottom:0.5px solid var(--accent);padding-bottom:0.25rem;align-self:center;transition:color 0.2s} .btn-secondary:hover{color:var(--text)} .hero-right{height:100vh;overflow:hidden;position:relative} .hero-right img{width:100%;height:100%;object-fit:cover;object-position:center top;opacity:0;animation:fadeIn 1.2s 0.2s ease forwards} .hero-right::after{content:'';position:absolute;left:0;top:0;bottom:0;width:40%;background:linear-gradient(to right,var(--bg),transparent)} /* DIVIDER */ .ornament{display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;gap:1.2rem;padding:3rem 0;opacity:0.6} .ornament-line{height:0.5px;width:60px;background:var(--accent-warm)} .ornament-diamond{width:5px;height:5px;background:var(--accent-warm);transform:rotate(45deg)} /* ABOUT */ .about{max-width:1100px;margin:0 auto;padding:5rem 2rem 6rem;display:grid;grid-template-columns:1.1fr 0.9fr;gap:6rem;align-items:start} .about-text .eyebrow{font-size:0.7rem;letter-spacing:0.25em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent-warm);margin-bottom:1.2rem} .about-text h2{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(2.2rem,4vw,3.4rem);font-weight:300;line-height:1.1;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin-bottom:2.2rem} .about-text h2 em{font-style:italic;color:var(--accent)} .about-text p{font-size:1rem;color:var(--text-mid);margin-bottom:1.4rem;line-height:1.85} .about-text p strong{font-weight:500;color:var(--text)} .about-aside{padding-top:0.5rem} .fact-card{border-left:2px solid var(--accent-warm);padding:1.4rem 1.6rem;background:var(--cream);margin-bottom:1.2rem} .fact-card h4{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:1rem;font-weight:400;color:var(--text);margin-bottom:0.8rem} .fact-card ul{list-style:none} .fact-card li{font-size:0.88rem;color:var(--text-mid);padding:0.32rem 0;border-bottom:0.5px solid var(--rule);display:flex;align-items:baseline;gap:0.6rem} .fact-card li:last-child{border-bottom:none} .fact-card li::before{content:'—';color:var(--accent-warm);font-size:0.75rem;flex-shrink:0} /* PHOTO BAND */ .photo-band{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr 1fr;height:420px;overflow:hidden} .photo-band-item{overflow:hidden;position:relative} .photo-band-item img{width:100%;height:100%;object-fit:cover;object-position:center top;transition:transform 0.6s ease} .photo-band-item:hover img{transform:scale(1.04)} .photo-band-item.tall{grid-row:span 1} /* MUSIC / DARK */ .music-dark{background:var(--bg-dark);padding:6rem 2rem;color:#e8ddd0} .music-inner{max-width:1100px;margin:0 auto} .music-header{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:4rem;align-items:end;margin-bottom:4rem} .music-dark .eyebrow{font-size:0.7rem;letter-spacing:0.25em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent-warm);margin-bottom:1.2rem} .music-dark h2{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(2.2rem,4vw,3.4rem);font-weight:300;line-height:1.1;color:#e8ddd0} .music-dark h2 em{font-style:italic;color:var(--accent-warm)} .music-dark .intro{font-size:1rem;color:rgba(232,221,208,0.65);line-height:1.8} .instruments{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(190px,1fr));gap:1px;background:rgba(196,149,106,0.15)} .inst{background:var(--bg-dark);padding:2.2rem 2rem;border:0.5px solid rgba(196,149,106,0.15);transition:background 0.3s,border-color 0.3s} .inst:hover{background:rgba(196,149,106,0.06);border-color:rgba(196,149,106,0.4)} .inst-name{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:1.7rem;font-weight:300;font-style:italic;color:#e8ddd0;display:block;margin-bottom:0.4rem} .inst-level{font-size:0.7rem;letter-spacing:0.15em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent-warm);display:block;margin-bottom:0.9rem} .inst-bar{height:1px;background:rgba(196,149,106,0.2);position:relative;margin-bottom:0.9rem} .inst-fill{height:1px;background:var(--accent-warm);transition:width 1.2s 0.3s ease} .inst-note{font-size:0.85rem;color:rgba(232,221,208,0.5);line-height:1.65} /* PULLQUOTE */ .pullquote-wrap{max-width:1100px;margin:0 auto;padding:5rem 2rem} .pullquote{border-left:2px solid var(--accent-warm);padding:2rem 3rem;background:var(--cream)} .pullquote p{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(1.2rem,2.5vw,1.75rem);font-weight:300;font-style:italic;color:var(--text);line-height:1.5;margin-bottom:1rem} .pullquote cite{font-size:0.72rem;letter-spacing:0.18em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--text-light);font-style:normal} /* EXPERIENCE */ .experience{max-width:1100px;margin:0 auto;padding:5rem 2rem} .experience .eyebrow{font-size:0.7rem;letter-spacing:0.25em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent-warm);margin-bottom:1.2rem} .experience h2{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(2.2rem,4vw,3.4rem);font-weight:300;line-height:1.1;margin-bottom:3rem} .experience h2 em{font-style:italic;color:var(--accent)} .timeline-item{display:grid;grid-template-columns:180px 1fr;gap:2.5rem;padding:2.5rem 0;border-bottom:0.5px solid var(--rule)} .timeline-item:last-child{border-bottom:none} .t-date{font-size:0.75rem;letter-spacing:0.12em;color:var(--accent-warm);text-transform:uppercase;padding-top:0.2rem;line-height:1.6} .t-content h3{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:1.3rem;font-weight:400;margin-bottom:0.2rem} .t-org{font-size:0.75rem;letter-spacing:0.12em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--text-light);margin-bottom:0.8rem} .t-content p{font-size:0.93rem;color:var(--text-mid);line-height:1.8} /* EDUCATION */ .education{background:var(--cream);padding:6rem 2rem;border-top:0.5px solid var(--rule)} .edu-inner{max-width:1100px;margin:0 auto} .education .eyebrow{font-size:0.7rem;letter-spacing:0.25em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent-warm);margin-bottom:1.2rem} .education h2{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(2.2rem,4vw,3.4rem);font-weight:300;line-height:1.1;margin-bottom:3rem} .education h2 em{font-style:italic;color:var(--accent)} .edu-grid{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(250px,1fr));gap:1.5rem} .edu-card{background:white;border:0.5px solid var(--rule);padding:1.8rem;position:relative;overflow:hidden;transition:box-shadow 0.3s} .edu-card:hover{box-shadow:0 4px 24px rgba(122,92,58,0.1)} .edu-card::after{content:'';position:absolute;bottom:0;left:0;width:100%;height:2px;background:var(--accent-warm);transform:scaleX(0);transform-origin:left;transition:transform 0.4s ease} .edu-card:hover::after{transform:scaleX(1)} .edu-badge{font-size:0.66rem;letter-spacing:0.15em;text-transform:uppercase;background:var(--accent-pale);color:var(--accent);padding:0.22rem 0.65rem;display:inline-block;margin-bottom:1rem} .edu-card h3{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:1.15rem;font-weight:400;margin-bottom:0.25rem} .edu-card .edu-sub{font-size:0.8rem;color:var(--text-light);margin-bottom:0.7rem} .edu-card p{font-size:0.88rem;color:var(--text-mid);line-height:1.65} /* GALLERY ROW */ .gallery-2{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;height:380px;overflow:hidden} .gallery-2 .g-item{overflow:hidden} .gallery-2 .g-item img{width:100%;height:100%;object-fit:cover;object-position:center top;transition:transform 0.6s ease} .gallery-2 .g-item:hover img{transform:scale(1.05)} /* CONTACT */ .contact{text-align:center;padding:7rem 2rem;background:var(--bg-dark);color:#e8ddd0} .contact .eyebrow{font-size:0.7rem;letter-spacing:0.25em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent-warm);margin-bottom:1.5rem} .contact h2{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(2.5rem,5vw,4.5rem);font-weight:300;line-height:1.05;letter-spacing:-0.01em;color:#e8ddd0;margin-bottom:1rem} .contact h2 em{font-style:italic;color:var(--accent-warm)} .contact .lede{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:1.1rem;font-style:italic;color:rgba(232,221,208,0.6);max-width:440px;margin:0 auto 3rem;line-height:1.7} .contact-email{display:inline-block;font-family:var(--serif);font-size:1.5rem;font-style:italic;color:var(--accent-warm);text-decoration:none;border-bottom:0.5px solid rgba(196,149,106,0.4);padding-bottom:0.25rem;margin-bottom:3rem;transition:opacity 0.2s} .contact-email:hover{opacity:0.7} .contact-links{display:flex;gap:2rem;justify-content:center;flex-wrap:wrap} .contact-link{font-size:0.72rem;letter-spacing:0.2em;text-transform:uppercase;text-decoration:none;color:rgba(232,221,208,0.55);border-bottom:0.5px solid rgba(232,221,208,0.2);padding-bottom:0.2rem;transition:color 0.2s,border-color 0.2s} .contact-link:hover{color:var(--accent-warm);border-color:var(--accent-warm)} /* FOOTER */ footer{text-align:center;padding:2rem;font-size:0.7rem;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:var(--text-light);border-top:0.5px solid var(--rule);text-transform:uppercase} /* REVEAL */ .reveal{opacity:0;transform:translateY(26px);transition:opacity 0.85s ease,transform 0.85s ease} .reveal.visible{opacity:1;transform:none} .reveal-delay-1{transition-delay:0.1s} .reveal-delay-2{transition-delay:0.2s} .reveal-delay-3{transition-delay:0.3s} /* ANIMATIONS */ @keyframes fadeUp{from{opacity:0;transform:translateY(20px)}to{opacity:1;transform:none}} @keyframes fadeIn{from{opacity:0}to{opacity:1}} /* RESPONSIVE */ @media(max-width:900px){ nav{padding:1.2rem 1.5rem} nav.scrolled{padding:0.8rem 1.5rem} .hero{grid-template-columns:1fr;min-height:auto} .hero-left{padding:7rem 1.5rem 3rem} .hero-right{height:60vw;min-height:320px} .hero-right::after{display:none} .about{grid-template-columns:1fr;gap:3rem;padding:4rem 1.5rem} .music-header{grid-template-columns:1fr;gap:1.5rem} .photo-band{grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;height:340px} .photo-band-item:last-child{display:none} .timeline-item{grid-template-columns:1fr;gap:0.4rem} .gallery-2{height:260px} .experience,.pullquote-wrap{padding:4rem 1.5rem} .education{padding:4rem 1.5rem} .music-dark{padding:4rem 1.5rem} .contact{padding:5rem 1.5rem} } @media(max-width:600px){ .nav-links{display:none} .hero h1{font-size:3.2rem} .photo-band{grid-template-columns:1fr;height:280px} .photo-band-item:nth-child(2),.photo-band-item:nth-child(3){display:none} .gallery-2{grid-template-columns:1fr;height:auto} .gallery-2 .g-item{height:260px} }

Musician & Multi-Instrumentalist

TayaBrownbill

Playing piano since the age of three. Studying Music at the University of York from autumn 2026 — with ambitions in music, education, and wellbeing.

Get in touch Learn more

About

Music as a way of connecting

I started playing piano at three years old, and music has been at the centre of everything since. Over the years that early love grew into something much wider — clarinet, saxophone, singing, bass guitar, and a deep fascination with what music does to people.

It was a day spent with Our Dementia Choir, as part of a placement with Nottinghamshire's Inspire Music, that gave me real clarity on the direction I want to go. Watching music reach people in ways that words can't — that stayed with me. It's shaped everything from the modules I'm excited to study at York, to the work I do now as a teaching assistant.

I'm a sixth-former at The Minster School, Southwell, heading to the University of York in autumn 2026 to study Music — with an interest in music psychology, ethnomusicology, and the ways music intersects with health and wellbeing. Right now I'm working towards Grade 8 in both clarinet and piano, conducting school ensembles, and supporting music education across Nottinghamshire with Inspire Culture.

Key strengths

  • Natural leader & ensemble conductor
  • Warm, people-focused communicator
  • Strong sight-reader across instruments
  • Dedicated & highly reliable
  • Analytical — music theory & musicology

Ensembles & roles

  • Training Orchestra — conductor
  • Jazz Band — director
  • Concert Band — director
  • Pit band — four school musicals
  • Performing Arts Leader (Yr 12–13)

Wider activities

  • Duke of Edinburgh — Bronze & Silver
  • Annual Handel's Messiah, Southwell Minster
  • Jess Gillam masterclass invitee
  • School piano competition — winner ×2

"After speaking to members of Our Dementia Choir, it fundamentally changed my perspective on the impact of music — I've always wanted a career related to music, teaching, and helping others, but not until having those conversations did I truly realise how important this was."

Personal Statement, 2025

Instruments

Playing across many voices

From the intimacy of a piano lesson at age three to performing baritone saxophone in a full school musical, Taya's musical range runs deep — and keeps growing.

Clarinet Working towards Grade 8 · Summer 2026

Grade 6 Merit (ABRSM). Pit band across four school musicals. Performed in Minster masterclass with Jess Gillam.

Piano Working towards Grade 8 · Autumn 2026

Grade 6 Merit (ABRSM). School piano competition winner twice. Playing since age three.

Saxophone Alto · Tenor · Bari · Self-taught extended

Learnt Tenor Sax and Baritone Sax to Grade 6+ standard for Shrek the Musical. Community and ensemble performance.

Voice Grade 5 Merit (ABRSM)

Sings tenor and alto in choral works. Annual Handel's Messiah performer at Southwell Minster.

Bass Guitar Self-taught

Arranged and performed in a skiffle band for One Man Two Guvnors — entirely student-led.

Music Theory Grade 5 Distinction (ABRSM)

Strong analytical foundation. A-level musicology developed through score analysis, composition history, and dictation.

Experience

Where music meets people

July 2025 — Present

Volunteer Teaching Assistant

Inspire Culture — Nottingham Music Hub, Newark on Trent

Supporting professional instrumental tutors delivering music education to children and young people across Nottinghamshire. Assists with lessons, demonstrates technique, and provides one-to-one encouragement. Spent a formative day with Our Dementia Choir — observing first-hand the impact of music on mental health and memory. Consistently praised by Inspire staff for warmth, reliability, and a natural way with learners. Skills developed include safeguarding awareness, inclusive teaching, and age-appropriate communication of musical concepts.

April 2023

School Work Experience — Teaching Assistant

Lowe's Wong Junior School, Southwell

Supported classroom teachers with primary-age pupils across small-group activities, reading support, and classroom routines. Confirmed a genuine enjoyment of working with children and reinforced the interest in a teaching-related career.

2022 — 2023

Community Volunteer

Reach Learning, Southwell

Regular community volunteering including horticultural and kitchen maintenance tasks. Achieved five-star feedback from the site manager for reliability and attitude.

Ongoing

Ensemble Leader & Performing Arts Lead

The Minster School, Southwell

Conducts and leads three school ensembles — a training orchestra for younger years, a jazz band, and a concert band. As Performing Arts Leader, organises ticket sales and coordinates competitions for junior students. Pit musician across four school musicals, including learning three new instruments to performance standard in a matter of months for Shrek the Musical.

Education

Qualifications & training

A Levels · Predicted

Music (A) · English Lit (A) · Drama (A)

The Minster School, Southwell · 2024–2026

Analytical depth across three creative disciplines. Musicology, score analysis, and composition history in Music; critical interpretation in English; directing and devising in Drama.

GCSEs · 2024

9 Maths · 9 English · 8 Science · 8 Music

The Minster School, Southwell

Strong across sciences and humanities alongside the arts. Full suite including Drama (7), Spanish (7), RE (7), and Further Maths (6).

ABRSM · Clarinet

Grade 6 Merit — working towards Grade 8

Target: Summer 2026

Primary instrument. Performed in Minster masterclass with saxophonist Jess Gillam. Extensive ensemble and pit band experience across multiple genres.

ABRSM · Piano

Grade 6 Merit — working towards Grade 8

Target: Autumn 2026

Playing since age three. Won the school piano competition twice. A continuously developed second instrument alongside clarinet.

ABRSM · Theory

Music Theory Grade 5 — Distinction

ABRSM, 2021

Strong theoretical foundation underpinning all performance and compositional work. Supports A-level musicology and score analysis.

University

BMus Music — University of York

Commencing Autumn 2026

Looking forward to modules in music psychology and ethnomusicology. Keen to explore how music connects with health, wellbeing, and community — and to develop performance alongside likeminded musicians.

Get in touch

Let's connect
